content:
# Adsorption Experiments

**Summary**: A method to study the removal of solutes from a liquid phase by an adsorbent.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009]] performed batch adsorption experiments to evaluate the impact of pH, time, and dosage on metal removal.

- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] used adsorption-experiments to evaluate the removal of MTBE from water using fly ash.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] used adsorption-experiments to evaluate the removal of Ni2+, Co2+, Cd2+, and Cr3+ from water.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] performed adsorption experiments in both batch and fixed-bed modes to evaluate sulfur removal.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] performed adsorption experiments to evaluate the capacity of AgNP, Fe2O3, and nZVI for mercury removal.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] performed adsorption experiments to evaluate the impact of pH, time, and dosage on cadmium removal.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] used adsorption experiments to evaluate the removal efficiency of phenol and cadmium using GPDADMAC and GPHM.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] performed batch sorption experiments to evaluate arsenic removal.

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] conducted fixed-bed column experiments to evaluate MTBE and BTEX removal.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014]] used adsorption-experiments to evaluate the removal of MTBE from water using fly ash.

- [[2015-musa|Musa, 2015]] used adsorption-experiments to evaluate the removal of Ni2+, Co2+, Cd2+, and Cr3+ from water.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] performed adsorption experiments in both batch and fixed-bed modes to evaluate sulfur removal.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] performed adsorption experiments to evaluate the capacity of AgNP, Fe2O3, and nZVI for mercury removal.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] performed adsorption experiments to evaluate the impact of pH, time, and dosage on cadmium removal.

- [[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019]] used adsorption experiments to evaluate the removal efficiency of phenol and cadmium using GPDADMAC and GPHM.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] performed batch sorption experiments to evaluate arsenic removal.

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] conducted fixed-bed column experiments to evaluate MTBE and BTEX removal.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Batch trials showed that increasing the adsorbent dose provided more binding sites for heavy metals. ([[2009-al-khaldi|Al-Khaldi, 2009, p. 65]])

- The study evaluates how parameters like pH, dosage, and contact time affect adsorption efficiency. ([[2014-adebayo|Adebayo, 2014, p. 18]])

- The experimental data fitted Langmuir, Freundlich, and Temkin isotherms for heavy metal adsorption. ([[2015-musa|Musa, 2015, p. 13]])

- Batch mode was used for initial assessment, while fixed-bed systems were used for breakthrough studies. ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 64]])

- AgNP showed high efficiency in removing Hg(II) under various conditions including pH and dosage. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 90]])

- The study of the effect of solution conditions such as pH, contact time, dosage... on the adsorption efficiency. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 33]])

- GPDADMAC achieved 80% removal of 10 mg/l phenol, while GPHM removed over 60% of 0.5 ppm cadmium. ([[2019-salawudeen|Salawudeen, 2019, p. 15]])

- Fe-modified biochar achieved 99% of arsenic removal in batch studies. ([[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021, p. 13]])

- The optimum treatment conditions for MTBE were found at a CAC dosage of 2 g, initial concentration of 5000 μg/L, flow rate of 1 mL/min, using sandstone bed type and bed height of 20 cm. ([[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025, p. 13]])
